full_text:
3-day alert for dust storms and rain in Rajasthan:Jaisalmer, Barmer hottest; Above-normal rainfall expected in May
- Copy link
Rajasthan is unlikely to see extreme heat in May. Due to active western disturbances in the first two weeks, dust storms and light rain are expected. Temperatures are likely to remain near normal. A yellow alert for rain has been issued in 19 districts on May 2, which will remain in effect till May 4. In the last 24 hours, some areas saw dust storms and hail along with rain.
Day heat, evening storms and rain likely Most places will see heat during the day, followed by dust storms and light rain in the evening. Weather conditions may continue like this for the next few days, giving some relief from heatwave conditions.
High temperatures recorded in western districts On Thursday, maximum temperatures of 42–43°C were recorded in Bikaner, Churu, Phalodi and Pilani. Barmer and Jaisalmer were the hottest at 44°C. Some districts like Sikar, Jaipur and Alwar also received light rain later in the day.
What’s next? According to Meteorological Centre Jaipur Director Radheshyam Sharma, another western disturbance will remain active for the next 4–5 days. Dust storms with wind speeds of 40–50 kmph and light rain are likely in some areas. Temperatures are expected to stay around normal, bringing relief from severe heat.
How was the weather in major cities
Jaipur: Hot day, storm in evening Jaipur saw strong sunshine and heat during the day, with a maximum temperature of 40.8°C. Weather changed after noon with clouds and dust storms. Light drizzle was recorded in the evening. Similar conditions are expected for the next two to three days.
Udaipur: Temperature steady at 40°C Udaipur recorded a maximum temperature of 40°C for the second consecutive day. The minimum temperature remained around 25°C. Daytime heat remained intense, creating heatwave-like conditions.
Ajmer: Relief from evening winds Ajmer recorded a maximum of 39.8°C and a minimum of 23.6°C. People faced heat and humidity during the day. Evening winds brought some relief.
Alwar: Clouds and light rain Alwar saw temperatures above 40°C during the day. Clouds developed in the afternoon, followed by strong winds and light drizzle, bringing some relief.
Jodhpur: Heat continues, arrangements for animals Jodhpur recorded a maximum temperature of 40.5°C and a minimum of 26.2°C. With rising heat, arrangements like coolers, fountains and water troughs have been made for animals.
Sikar: Drizzle after afternoon heat Sikar recorded a maximum of 39°C and a minimum of 24.5°C. After intense heat in the afternoon, strong winds and light drizzle were seen in several areas.
Kota: Evening rain after hot day Kota recorded a maximum temperature of 40°C and a minimum of 21.4°C. After a hot day, strong winds and light rain occurred in the evening. Water arrangements have been made at several places to provide relief from heat.
Overall trend Rajasthan will continue to see hot days, but changing weather in the evening is likely to prevent extreme heat conditions in early May.
